dict.string: FloatingClock uses your approximate location to compute accurate sunrise, sunset, and civil-twilight times for the dawn/day/dusk/night glyph. City-level accuracy only — no precise GPS, no network, no data leaves your Mac.

FloatingClock uses your approximate location to compute accurate sunrise, sunset, and civil-twilight times for the dawn/day/dusk/night glyph. City-level accuracy only — no precise GPS, no network, no data leaves your Mac.

FloatingClock lists your paired Bluetooth headphones and speakers in the audio bar's right-click menu and connects the one you pick — including pulling it over from another device such as an iPhone. No scanning, no tracking; only devices you already paired.

FloatingClock measures your microphone's signal level (never records or transmits audio) to detect when the mic is muted — including a hardware mute button that macOS cannot otherwise see — and show a MUTED banner over the clock.
